title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Fashion and identity: does what you wear say who you are? Write an article exploring the relationship between clothing and personal identity, and share your own views on fashion today.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dressed to Express: What Your Clothes Say About You

Every morning, before we say a single word to anyone, we communicate through what we wear. Whether we choose a sharp suit, worn jeans, or a bright vintage dress, our clothing sends a message about who we are — or who we want to be perceived as.

Fashion has always been a form of self-expression. Teenagers in every generation have used clothing to signal their identity and separate themselves from the generation above them. Subcultures from punk to streetwear have built entire identities around distinctive styles.

However, fashion today faces serious challenges. Fast fashion — the production of cheap, disposable clothing — has made style more accessible but at enormous environmental and human cost. Many fashion brands rely on poorly paid workers in difficult conditions to produce garments that consumers discard after a handful of wears.

Personally, I believe we should think more carefully about what we buy and why. Choosing quality over quantity, or shopping second-hand, is both a style statement and an ethical one.

Wear what you love — but wear it thoughtfully.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'We want reviews of fashion apps, online shopping experiences, or clothing subscription services. Write a review of a fashion-related app or service you have used, describing what it offers and whether you would recommend it.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
App Review: Vinted — Sustainable Fashion Made Easy

Vinted is a popular online marketplace for buying and selling second-hand clothing, shoes, and accessories. With growing concerns about the environmental impact of fast fashion, apps like Vinted have become increasingly relevant — and for good reason.

Using the app is straightforward. You photograph your unwanted items, set a price, and list them in minutes. Buyers can browse thousands of items filtered by size, brand, and price range. The delivery and payment system is secure and reliable, and I have never had a problem with a transaction.

In terms of savings, I have bought designer items for a fraction of their original price, and sold clothes I no longer wear for a pleasing amount of extra cash. The community aspect is also surprisingly friendly — sellers respond promptly and packaging is usually thoughtful.

The only downside is that popular sizes sell out quickly, so you need to check the app regularly to find the best bargains.

Overall, Vinted is a brilliant way to refresh your wardrobe sustainably and affordably. Highly recommended. Rating: 4.5/5

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Alex has asked for advice because they are starting a new job and need to build a professional wardrobe on a limited budget. Write an email to Alex giving advice on how to dress professionally without spending too much money.

Write your email to Alex.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Alex,

Congratulations on the new job — how exciting! I completely understand the challenge of building a professional wardrobe on a budget. Here are a few ideas that worked really well for me when I was in the same situation.

The key is to invest in a small number of versatile basics rather than lots of trendy pieces. A few well-fitting neutral items — dark trousers or a skirt, a couple of smart shirts or blouses, and a good blazer — can be mixed and matched to create many different outfits. Think of it as building a capsule wardrobe.

For affordable options, I'd really recommend charity shops and second-hand apps like Vinted or Depop. You can find excellent quality items for a fraction of the original price, and it's much more sustainable too. End-of-season sales at high-street shops are also a great opportunity to pick up quality pieces cheaply.

Most importantly, make sure everything fits well — that makes the biggest difference of all!

Good luck with the new role!

Lots of love,
Sophie
